calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Jan-18-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PETITIONER ANTHONY SINGER's Writ Of Mandate.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Anthony Singer's "petition for a writ of mandate compelling production of public records" is denied.

Singer's petition "requests that the court issue a writ of mandate directing the San Francisco Police Department to produce specific records identified in attachment B." (Pet. 4:26-27.) Attachment B requests "public records" containing 18 types of information regarding the 525 people arrested by the police between June 8 and September 5, 2022. (See Opp. 3:10-12.) Singer relies on Government Code 6254(f) for his request and petition. The petition is denied for two reasons.

First, Government Code 6254(f) "does not authorize the release of 'records'," as Singer's petition demands, "but only 'information' extracted from the records." (County of Los Angeles v. Sup. Ct. (1993) 18 Cal.App.4th 588, 600-01.)

Second, Government Code 6254(f) is limited to "contemporaneous disclosure of individualized arrest information in order to prevent secret arrests" - not information regarding past arrests. (County of Los Angeles, 18 Cal.App.4th at 598.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
